response.justification: Bogdan brings 15+ years of genuine full-stack depth — Node.js, TypeScript, React, MongoDB, Docker, Kubernetes, AWS CDK — plus directly relevant AI agent and MCP framework work at re:cinq that matches the job's AI agent development requirement. His TDD culture is unusually strong: he built testing libraries, ran dojos, and organized formal courses. The main gaps are Nest.js (the core backend framework, never mentioned), Kotlin (secondary requirement, absent), Python (listed as required but absent from his skills), and no explicit Vitest/Playwright references. These are real gaps in the specified stack but learnable for an engineer of his breadth; the fundamentals and engineering discipline are clearly there.
strengths: Active AI-agent and MCP infrastructure work at current role directly maps to the job's AI agent development requirement
Deep, demonstrated TDD culture — authored D-language testing frameworks, organized code katas and Selenium courses, applied TDD/pairing/CI across multiple roles

Genuine full-stack breadth across 15+ years: Node.js, TypeScript, React, MongoDB, MySQL, AWS CDK, Docker, Kubernetes, plus mobile and embedded — validates the 'solid engineering and architecture skills' requirement
concerns: Nest.js — the primary backend framework in the job's stack — appears nowhere on the resume; adoption curve is non-trivial for an experienced engineer used to other paradigms
Python is listed as a required proficiency in the job description but is absent from both the skills section and all role descriptions
Kotlin (secondary stack requirement) is not mentioned; Java experience exists but is dated and limited to tooling/robotics contexts rather than production service development
